Package neqsim.process.automation

Class TransactionalBatchResult.WriteOutcome

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.automation.TransactionalBatchResult.WriteOutcome
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
Enclosing class:
TransactionalBatchResult
public static final class TransactionalBatchResult.WriteOutcome extends Object implements Serializable
Per-write outcome inside a transactional batch.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    WriteOutcome(String address, double requestedValue, String unit, Double previousValue, WriteValidationResult validation, boolean applied, String error)
    Creates a write outcome.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    getAddress()
    Returns the address that was requested.
    String
    getError()
    Returns the error message if the apply phase threw an exception for this write, otherwise null.
    Double
    getPreviousValue()
    Returns the value read from the simulation before the batch began, or null when the value was unreadable (for example a new INPUT variable that had not yet been set).
    double
    getRequestedValue()
    Returns the value that was requested.
    String
    getUnit()
    Returns the unit of measure of getRequestedValue(), or null for the default.
    WriteValidationResult
    getValidation()
    Returns the validation result for this write; never null.
    boolean
    isApplied()
    Returns whether the write was actually pushed into the simulation.
    com.google.gson.JsonObject
    toJson()
    Renders this outcome as a JSON object.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Field Details

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • address

      private final String address

    • requestedValue

      private final double requestedValue

    • unit

      private final String unit

    • previousValue

      private final Double previousValue

    • validation

      private final WriteValidationResult validation

    • applied

      private final boolean applied

    • error

      private final String error

  • Constructor Details

    • WriteOutcome

      public WriteOutcome(String address, double requestedValue, String unit, Double previousValue, WriteValidationResult validation, boolean applied, String error)
      Creates a write outcome.
      Parameters:
      address - the address that was requested
      requestedValue - the value that was requested
      unit - the unit of measure of requestedValue, or null for the default
      previousValue - the value read before the batch began, or null when unreadable
      validation - the validation result for this write; never null
      applied - true if the write was actually pushed into the simulation
      error - the error message if the apply phase threw, otherwise null

  • Method Details

    • getAddress

      public String getAddress()
      Returns the address that was requested.
      Returns:
      the address

    • getRequestedValue

      public double getRequestedValue()
      Returns the value that was requested.
      Returns:
      the requested value

    • getUnit

      public String getUnit()
      Returns the unit of measure of getRequestedValue(), or null for the default.
      Returns:
      the unit or null

    • getPreviousValue

      public Double getPreviousValue()
      Returns the value read from the simulation before the batch began, or null when the value was unreadable (for example a new INPUT variable that had not yet been set).
      Returns:
      the previous value or null

    • getValidation

      public WriteValidationResult getValidation()
      Returns the validation result for this write; never null.
      Returns:
      the validation result

    • isApplied

      public boolean isApplied()
      Returns whether the write was actually pushed into the simulation. A write may pass validation and still be false when an earlier write in the same batch failed.
      Returns:
      true if the write was applied

    • getError

      public String getError()
      Returns the error message if the apply phase threw an exception for this write, otherwise null.
      Returns:
      the error or null

    • toJson

      public com.google.gson.JsonObject toJson()
      Renders this outcome as a JSON object.
      Returns:
      the JSON object
